untranslatable_reason: High Tight Flag (HTF) pattern detection requires geometric pattern recognition (trend-line boundary identification, flag shape analysis, minor low tracking) which cannot be expressed using only the allowed point-in-time indicators. The signal references HTF trend-line boundaries, irregular shape detection, and dynamic stop placement relative to prior minor lows—all pattern-specific concepts that require candlestick pattern analysis not available in the indicator set.
translation_notes: This signal is fundamentally pattern-based and requires identifying the HTF geometric structure (parallel trend lines, flag body, breakout level). While a simple breakout above prior high can approximate entry, and a close below prior low can approximate exit, these do not capture the true HTF mechanics including trend-line validation, pattern confirmation, and the critical 'raise stop to just below prior minor low' rule which requires historical pattern context.
